unit.note: All 28 new tests (test_audit_compliance_on_stop.py) + the new versioned-path regression (test_audit_phase_quality.py::test_phase_from_plugin_root_versioned_install) pass. Full shared/tests: 2587 passed, 14 skipped, 2 failed. BOTH failures are external to this change: (1) test_artifact_path_canon[compliance-migrated] — pre-existing canon-lint false-positive on ADR slug 'compliance/rtm-status-logic' in a prior iterate's committed JSON (commit 76a3b12 / PR #115); (2) test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact — caused solely by TWO other iterates' decision drops (gitignore-canon-propagation, reviewer-stack, impact=convention) sitting in the shared, gitignored main-tree decision-drops staging; LOCAL-ONLY (decision drops are not committed, so this cannot occur in CI). My own decision drop is impact=none and is not flagged. See degraded[].

e2e.note: Empirical hook E2E against the real worktree project: (1) full A-G audit emitted real compliance fails + idempotent skip on re-run; (2) injected a resolved compliance item -> hook reported dismissed=1 and flipped it to dismissed/auditResolved while genuinely-failing items stayed in triage; (3) RUNTIME-FAITHFUL re-test: invoked with the REAL versioned CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT (.../shipwright-iterate/0.4.1, per installed_plugins.json) -> hook fired (appended=5) after the phase_from_plugin_root version-tolerance fix, whereas before the fix it (and phase_quality) silently no-opped; (4) reactivation backlog measured empirically: re-enabled phase_quality emitted at most ~1 per-phase Tier-1 FAIL (deduped, non-blocking), not a flood. No web/UI surface.

surface_verification.justification: Backend Stop-hook triage producer (shared/scripts/hooks/audit_compliance_on_stop.py) + two hooks.json Stop-chain wirings + docs. No startable web/CLI/API app surface is affected, and the triage.jsonl contract/schema is unchanged (this only adds a new automatic trigger for the existing audit_detector.mirror_findings_to_triage producer). Verification is the 28 hook unit/integration tests plus empirical hook E2E (emit, auto-dismiss, full-coverage safety gate, idempotency) run directly against the real worktree project.

degraded.detail: shared/tests/test_artifact_path_canon.py::test_no_legacy_artifact_paths[compliance-migrated] fails on the origin/main base (commit 76a3b12, PR #115) — a canon-lint false-positive matching 'compliance/' inside the ADR slug value 'compliance/rtm-status-logic' in a prior iterate's recorded JSON. Out of scope for this iterate (a different iterate's append-only audit record; JSON cannot carry an inline canon marker). Recommend a follow-up to either narrow the canon regex for ADR-slug contexts or extend ALLOWLIST['compliance'].

test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact fails ONLY because two OTHER iterates' decision drops (gitignore-canon-propagation, reviewer-stack; impact=convention) sit in the shared, gitignored main-tree decision-drops staging without architecture.md entries. Local-dev-machine state only — decision drops are not committed, so this cannot reproduce in CI. My own drop was reclassified impact=none (a pipeline producer-trigger + resolver bugfix, documented in hooks-and-pipeline.md, not an app-level architecture change), so it is not flagged.

Autonomous run; medium+ external LLM review skipped (no API keys available). Compensated by 28 targeted tests, the full shared/tests suite, and direct empirical hook E2E proving emit/auto-dismiss/idempotency and the full-coverage safety gate.
